title: Secure show authoring and live production
scope: The OSS Studio, local authentication, show and scene authoring, reusable components, Preview and Program operation, OBS outputs, and Linux production control integrations.
summary: OverlayKit OSS is a single-instance, secure-by-default production studio in which authenticated users create Shows, compose Scenes from reusable Components or Templates, prepare content in Preview, promote it to Program, render Program through tokenized OBS outputs, and operate the same typed controls from Studio or Linux-compatible control adapters.

actors.label: Owner
actors.description: Bootstraps and secures the local instance, manages users, outputs, and integrations.

actors.label: Designer
actors.description: Creates scenes, components, templates, and operator-facing control definitions.

actors.label: Producer
actors.description: Prepares Preview, operates controls, and decides what is promoted to Program.

actors.label: Linux operator
actors.description: Controls production from a Stream Deck-compatible Linux workflow without depending on the official Elgato desktop application.

terms.name: Show
terms.definition: The top-level user workspace that owns scenes, production state, outputs, and show-local configuration.

terms.name: Scene
terms.definition: A saved source document describing a composition of component instances.

terms.name: Component Definition
terms.definition: A reusable, versioned visual behavior with declared properties and operator controls.

terms.name: Component Instance
terms.definition: A version-pinned placement of a component definition inside a scene with scene-specific values and geometry.

terms.name: Template
terms.definition: An immutable starter that creates an independent scene or component definition when selected.

terms.name: Preview
terms.definition: A mutable runtime snapshot prepared off air for inspection and adjustment.

terms.name: Program
terms.definition: The authoritative on-air runtime snapshot rendered by live outputs.

terms.name: Take
terms.definition: The explicit command that atomically promotes the prepared Preview snapshot to Program.

terms.name: Control Definition
terms.definition: A typed and labeled operator capability declared by a component, such as text, number, toggle, selection, color, or action.

terms.name: Output
terms.definition: A read-only, tokenized rendering endpoint for OBS or another browser-source consumer.

requirements.statement: Show is the top-level user-visible workspace and owns scenes, production state, outputs, and show-local settings.
requirements.verification: Domain model tests and route-level authorization tests prove every owned resource resolves through a Show.

requirements.statement: A Scene is a saved source document and remains distinct from Preview and Program runtime snapshots.
requirements.verification: Persistence and production state-machine tests prove independent scene and runtime revisions.

requirements.statement: Component Definitions are reusable and versioned; each Scene stores version-pinned Component Instances.
requirements.verification: Component library tests prove a later definition revision does not silently change an existing scene instance.

requirements.statement: Selecting a Template creates an independent Scene or Component Definition and never mutates the Template.
requirements.verification: Template clone tests compare source and derived resource hashes before and after edits.

requirements.statement: Collection is removed from the user vocabulary and channel identifiers remain internal transport details.
requirements.verification: UI copy, public route, and API contract tests contain no collection or channel workflow exposed to users.

requirements.statement: Components declare typed Control Definitions; Studio and integrations consume the same definitions and allowed commands.
requirements.verification: Contract tests generate equivalent Studio and integration action catalogs from one component definition.

requirements.statement: Shows, Production, Scenes, Editor, Library, Security, Outputs, and Integrations are surfaces within one authenticated Studio shell.
requirements.verification: Router tests and responsive navigation E2E tests cover the complete application hierarchy.

requirements.statement: Opening a Show leads to Production by default, with Scenes and Show Settings available as sibling destinations.
requirements.verification: Navigation E2E tests assert the default route and stable show context.

requirements.statement: Editor presents library, canvas, and inspector as one composition workflow and uses Send to Preview as its primary production command.
requirements.verification: Editor workflow E2E tests create, save, and send a scene without changing applications or exposing channel identifiers.

requirements.statement: Production presents a scene rundown, Preview and Program monitors, an explicit Take command, and contextual component controls on one surface.
requirements.verification: Production workflow E2E tests complete load, adjust, take, and visibility operations from one route.

requirements.statement: First run requires creation of a local Owner, and Studio has no anonymous authenticated-area fallback.
requirements.verification: First-run and route-guard integration tests reject Studio access until owner setup and login complete.

requirements.statement: Interactive access uses server-managed secure sessions and role checks for Owner, Producer, and Designer capabilities.
requirements.verification: Session, CSRF, logout, expiry, and role-matrix integration tests.

requirements.statement: OBS Outputs use revocable, rotatable, read-only tokens and never receive an interactive user session.
requirements.verification: Output authorization tests prove read-only scope, rotation invalidation, and absence of session credentials.

requirements.statement: Control devices use revocable tokens restricted to a Show and explicit action scopes.
requirements.verification: Device authorization tests reject cross-show, undeclared, expired, and revoked actions.

requirements.statement: The default server binding is local-only; LAN exposure requires explicit configuration and secure proxy guidance.
requirements.verification: Configuration tests assert the default bind address and deployment documentation check.

requirements.statement: Production maintains explicit Preview and Program snapshots with independently observable revisions.
requirements.verification: State-machine and websocket tests assert isolated revisions and reconnect snapshots.

requirements.statement: Take atomically promotes the complete Preview snapshot to Program and exposes success or failure to every operator.
requirements.verification: Concurrent production tests prove atomic promotion, idempotency, and shared acknowledgement.

requirements.statement: Every runtime mutation names Preview or Program; Preview is the default and direct Program mode is an explicit Show setting.
requirements.verification: Command validation and production UI tests reject ambiguous targets and verify direct-mode configuration.

requirements.statement: Runtime edits never alter a saved Scene unless an authorized user executes an explicit save-back command.
requirements.verification: Persistence tests compare saved scene revisions across runtime operations and explicit save-back.

requirements.statement: A vendor-neutral Control API publishes a typed action catalog, executes authorized commands, and streams state feedback.
requirements.verification: Control API contract tests cover discovery, execution, authorization, idempotency, and feedback.

requirements.statement: The first Linux Stream Deck path is a Bitfocus Companion adapter over the Control API; hardware-specific behavior does not enter the core runtime.
requirements.verification: Adapter contract tests run against the same catalog fixtures as Studio and contain no core dependency on an Elgato SDK.

userStories.need: bootstrap the instance with an owner identity
userStories.outcome: the Studio is secure before any project data can be accessed
preconditions: The instance has no registered owner.

acceptanceCriteria.when: a visitor requests any Studio route
acceptanceCriteria.then: the visitor is restricted to owner setup until valid credentials are created
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: first-run owner bootstrap and guarded routes.

acceptanceCriteria.then: the visitor cannot create another bootstrap owner
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Integration: setup endpoint closes after bootstrap.

userStories.need: sign in and end my session
userStories.outcome: only authenticated people can reach Studio capabilities

acceptanceCriteria.then: the server creates a secure session and redirects to Shows
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: login, session cookie attributes, and redirect.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the user signs out or the session expires
acceptanceCriteria.then: protected requests are rejected and the session cannot be reused
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Integration: logout and expiry invalidate the server session.

userStories.need: create, open, duplicate, and archive Shows
userStories.outcome: each production has a clear workspace and lifecycle
preconditions: The producer has Show management permission.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the producer creates a Show with valid metadata
acceptanceCriteria.then: OverlayKit opens that Show on its Production surface with isolated resources
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: create Show and verify ownership boundaries.

userStories.need: create a Scene from a blank canvas or a Template
userStories.outcome: I can start authoring at the right level of reuse
preconditions: A Show exists and the designer can edit it.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the designer chooses blank or a Template
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new independent Scene is created in that Show and opens in Editor
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: blank and template scene creation.

userStories.outcome: the visual source remains editable and can be prepared for production

acceptanceCriteria.when: the designer adds, arranges, configures, and saves component instances
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Scene revision persists without changing Preview or Program
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: editor composition and isolated save.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the designer selects Send to Preview
acceptanceCriteria.then: Preview receives a runtime snapshot and Program remains unchanged

userStories.need: create or derive reusable Components and declare their controls
userStories.outcome: visual behavior can be reused safely across Scenes and operated without editing code
preconditions: The designer can manage the Component Library.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a custom selection or Component Template
acceptanceCriteria.when: the designer saves a Component Definition
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Library stores a versioned definition that can be inserted into Scenes
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: create, version, and reuse a component.

acceptanceCriteria.then: invalid control types and paths are rejected and valid controls appear in Production
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Contract and E2E: control schema validation and generated operator UI.

userStories.need: load Scenes in Preview, adjust their controls, and Take them to Program
userStories.outcome: I can operate a live Show from one predictable surface
preconditions: The Show has at least one saved Scene and the producer can operate it.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a saved Scene in the Production rundown
acceptanceCriteria.then: Preview renders the Scene and exposes only its declared controls
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: rundown load and contextual controls.

acceptanceCriteria.then: Program atomically matches Preview and all connected operators receive acknowledgement
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E and concurrency: Take promotion and feedback.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the producer leaves Production without save-back
acceptanceCriteria.then: the saved Scene revision remains unchanged
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Integration: runtime isolation from saved source.

userStories.need: create and rotate a protected OBS Output URL
userStories.outcome: OBS can render Program without access to Studio or write capabilities
preconditions: A Show exists and the owner can manage Outputs.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the owner creates an OBS Output
acceptanceCriteria.then: OverlayKit issues a read-only URL that renders Program with transparent output support
acceptanceCriteria.verification: E2E: tokenized OBS render and scope denial.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the owner rotates or revokes it
acceptanceCriteria.then: the previous URL stops receiving Program and cannot be reused
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Integration: output token rotation and revocation.

userStories.outcome: a controller can operate one Show without inheriting my user session
preconditions: The owner can manage Show integrations.

acceptanceCriteria.when: the owner creates a device token with selected action scopes
acceptanceCriteria.then: the token can execute only those actions for that Show and is shown only once

userStories.need: bind Stream Deck buttons to production actions and receive state feedback on Linux
userStories.outcome: I can control Scenes, Take, component visibility, values, and actions from my production desk
preconditions: Bitfocus Companion is available and a scoped OverlayKit device token exists.

acceptanceCriteria.then: the module lists the authorized Show, Scenes, controls, visibility commands, Take, and trigger actions
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Adapter contract: action discovery from the Control API catalog.

acceptanceCriteria.when: production state changes from Studio or another device
acceptanceCriteria.then: the button receives current state feedback and executes commands against its explicit target
acceptanceCriteria.verification: Adapter integration: bidirectional state and command execution.

workflows.title: Secure first run
steps: Open a fresh instance.
Create the initial Owner through the one-time setup surface.
Authenticate and enter the Shows surface.
Configure explicit LAN exposure only when required.

workflows.title: Author a reusable Scene
steps: Open or create a Show.
Create a blank Scene or derive one from a Template.
Compose version-pinned Component Instances in Editor.
Create reusable Component Definitions and typed controls when needed.
Save the Scene and send a runtime snapshot to Preview.

workflows.title: Operate Preview and Program
steps: Open the Show Production surface.
Load a saved Scene from the rundown into Preview.
Adjust declared controls and visibility in Preview.
Inspect Preview and execute Take.
Observe Program and connected Output health.
Optionally save runtime state back through an explicit authorized command.

workflows.title: Control production from Linux
steps: Create a Show-scoped device token with selected actions.
Configure the OverlayKit Companion adapter with that credential.
Discover actions and bind them to controller buttons.
Execute commands and receive state feedback from the Control API.
Rotate or revoke the device token when access changes.

outOfScope: Cloud multi-tenancy, hosted account management, billing, marketplace, trivia, and analytics.
An official Elgato plugin as the initial Linux integration path.
Automatic persistence of Preview or Program mutations into saved Scenes.
Exposing transport channel identifiers as a primary user workflow.
A public unauthenticated Studio, Editor, or Production control surface.
